reasoning_model_1: The model failed significantly in Content Accuracy by reading the target text twice (repetition/hallucination), which is a major violation of the script. Regarding Paralinguistic Compliance (Speech Rate), the model failed to follow the specific instruction to 'start slow'. The audio starts at a moderate-to-fast pace (00:00-00:03) and maintains a similar pace throughout the first iteration (ends at 00:07) and the second iteration. There is no noticeable dynamic adjustment from slow to fast; the entire output is delivered with a consistent, relatively energetic rhythm that ignores the 'start slow' constraint entirely.
reasoning_model_2: The model achieved perfect Content Accuracy, reading the script exactly once without errors. In terms of Paralinguistic Compliance (Speech Rate), the model successfully executed the first part of the instruction by starting with a deliberate, slow pace for the first sentence '同学们请注意，这项实验非常关键' (00:00-00:03). For the second part, '现在请大家立刻开始操作...' (00:04-00:08), the model increased the speech rate relative to the start. Although the resulting speed was more 'normal' than 'fast' (lacking a sense of extreme urgency), the direction of the change (acceleration) was correct and the initial slow state was accurate. This constitutes a partially correct execution where the magnitude of the change was insufficient.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 contains a critical content error by repeating the entire script, and it completely fails the paralinguistic instruction by starting fast and maintaining a constant speed, ignoring the 'start slow' requirement. Model 2 correctly follows the content script and attempts the dynamic adjustment: it successfully establishes the 'slow' start and transitions to a faster pace, even though the final speed was not as fast as requested. Model 2 demonstrates control over the dynamic feature, whereas Model 1 fails both content and style.
